ko.title: IRS CP23 Notice TY2022 — $74,280 Discrepancy Assessment
ko.description: Analysis of IRS CP23 notice asserting a $74,280 balance due for tax year 2022. Covers applicable statutory authority (26 U.S.C. §§ 6213, 6501), procedural rights, taxpayer response options, and the evidentiary steps required to verify or dispute the IRS assessment. Counter-evidence systematically assessed per BKGC Art. XIX.

claims.text: The IRS CP23 notice for TY2022 asserts a balance due of $74,280 based on a discrepancy between IRS records and the taxpayer's reported figures.
A CP23 is not a statutory Notice of Deficiency under 26 U.S.C. § 6212; the taxpayer retains the right to respond and dispute within 60 days before any Notice of Deficiency is issued.

The IRS three-year assessment limitations period under § 6501(a) for TY2022 opened approximately April 2023. Absent a § 6501(e) substantial omission or § 6501(c) fraud exception, the window closes approximately April 2026.

ko.governing_framework: BKGC v2.0 / BGS v1.0 / BRA v1.0
